document.diagnosis: Iteration 25 did not improve weakest-family accuracy: every reviewed mechanism bottomed out at 2/6 in at least one family. The five-bank three-review champion remained strongest at 10/18, with seven helpful and zero harmful interventions. Nine-bank selection exposed the main unresolved gap: both selector depths converted only 9 of 13 oracle-covered cases. Three selectors provided no exact gain over one selector, although partial accuracy favored three selectors, 81.8% versus 77.8%. Regeneration again improved coverage, from 10/18 to 12/18, but its integrator finished at 6/18 and caused two harmful reversals. The next batch should therefore replicate full-bank selection, preserve its one-call efficiency ablation, and retain regeneration as the orthogonal candidate-generation lane without introducing another voting variant.
extensions_requested: Add an `audit_then_choose` primitive: three independent reviewers examine the same shuffled, frequency-hidden candidate bank and write candidate-indexed falsification findings to a shared evidence board; one terminal choose-only selector sees the candidates and anonymized findings, cannot synthesize a new answer, and falls back to base plurality on invalid output. This directly tests whether explicit evidence aggregation closes the oracle-to-final gap better than reviewer plurality.
most_important_evidence: The retained five-bank three-review repair mechanism scored 10/18 with 33.3% weakest-family accuracy, seven helpful interventions, and zero harmful reversals.
The nine-bank oracle covered 13/18 cases, but both three-selector and one-selector systems finished at 9/18, leaving four covered cases unconverted.
Three selectors and one selector tied at 9/18 with identical 33.3% weakest-family accuracy; the deeper panel used two additional calls per case and led only in partial accuracy, 81.8% versus 77.8%.
Regenerate-then-repair raised coverage from 10/18 to 12/18 and introduced two correct candidates absent from the base bank, but converted only 6/18 and made two harmful reversals.
Five-vote plurality converted only 3/10 oracle-covered cases and scored 0/6 on sequence, confirming that deterministic plurality remains a control rather than a solution to candidate selection.

proposals.hypothesis: Replicate the exact full-bank candidate-only selector. Across Iterations 24 and 25 it has positive correction evidence, while another fresh panel is needed to determine whether its three-selector conversion advantage over repair is stable.
proposals.name: Nine-Bank Falsifying Selection Panel

proposals.hypothesis: Replicate the exact efficiency ablation after its Iteration 25 exact tie with three selectors. If the tie persists without a meaningful weakest-family or partial-accuracy loss, two selector calls per case can be removed.
proposals.name: Nine-Bank One-Selector Falsifying Selection

proposals.hypothesis: Retain the frozen orthogonal pipeline unchanged. Its recurring coverage gains remain valuable evidence about candidate generation, while replication must measure whether the recent harmful integration rate persists.
proposals.name: Blind Regeneration then Falsifying Integration

proposals.hypothesis: Use as the first reserve proposal if a retained mechanism occupies another slot. Sequential falsification tests whether passing an explicit proposal and critique between reviewers improves conversion differently from independent selector plurality.
proposals.name: Sequential Five-Bank Cross-Examination

research_hypotheses: The nine-bank selector's gain comes primarily from candidate-only adjudication, so one selector may preserve exact and weakest-family accuracy at lower cost.
Three-selector plurality may still be worthwhile if its Iteration 25 partial-accuracy advantage becomes an exact or weakest-family advantage under replication.
Blind diversified regeneration reliably improves oracle coverage, but terminal integration—not generation—is currently limiting its final accuracy.
A shared, candidate-indexed evidence board may improve selection more than additional independent votes because it exposes reasons for rejecting attractive wrong candidates.
document.risk_note: Assuming the Iteration 25 winner and pooled champion are the same retained five-bank repair mechanism, the six-strategy batch consists of three mechanical retentions plus the first three proposals. Its maximum cost is 240 calls: 108 shared bank calls and 132 review calls, safely below 300. Results will remain highly correlated through the shared banks and noisy over only 12 cases; the one-versus-three-selector tie should not trigger promotion or pruning from one additional panel alone.
